expressions.english: slow song
(someone) speaks slowly
(someone) walks slowly
one's reaction is slow
to be slow at understanding
to act slowly

one beat behind in responding or understanding
a little slow in responding or understanding
a little slow on the uptake
expressions.notes:
하나 = one (native Korean number)
한 is the adjective form of 하나.
박자 = tempo

expressions.english: Why is your reaction so slow?
Yoona speaks very slowly.
Seokjin is a little slow in understanding.
The Internet is really slow and I'm really annoyed.
expressions.notes: 짜증나다 = to be annoying; to be annoyed

expressions.korean: 컴퓨터가 너무 느려서 일을 하기 힘들 어요.
expressions.english: The computer is so slow that it's hard to work (with it).
expressions.notes:
컴퓨터 = computer
일을 하다 = to work

expressions.korean: 앞 차가 너무 느리게 가서 추월해 버렸 어요.
expressions.english: The car in front of me was driving really slow, so I passed it.
expressions.notes:
앞 = in front of; front
차 = car
추월하다 = to pass (a car)
-이/이/여 버리다 is used when talking about something that happened against your hope or expectations.

expressions.korean: KTX가 아무리 빨라도 비행기보다는 느 려요.
expressions.english: No matter how fast KTX is, it's slower than an airplane.
expressions.notes:
KTX = Korean Train eXpress
The fastest train running in Korea. Top speed for trains in regular service is currently 305 km/h (190 mph).
아무리 = no matter + adjective/adverb
빨르다 = to be fast
-이/이/여도 = even though; no matter how much + S + V
비행기 = airplane
-보다 = than + N

expressions.korean: 컴퓨터가 많이 느리면 메모리를 추가하 세요.
expressions.english: If your computer is too slow, add memory.
expressions.notes:
많다 = to be a lot
많이 is the adverb form of 많다.
메모리 = (computer) memory
추가하다 = to add

expressions.korean: 핸드폰을 4년 동안 썼더니 이제 많이 느려요.
expressions.english: I've been using my mobile phone for four years now, so it's really slow.
expressions.notes:
핸드폰 = mobile phone
4년 = four years
동안 = during, while; for + period of time
쓰다 = to use; to spend [+ money]
-았/었/였던 = since/because + I/we + V-ed
이제 = now

expressions.korean: 저는 겨울에는 이런 느린 노래를 듣는 걸 좋아해요.
expressions.english: I like listening to slow songs like this in the winter.
expressions.notes:
겨울 = winter
이런 = N + like this
듣다 = to hear; to listen
좋아하다 = to like

expressions.korean: 저는 걸음이 많이 느려서 어디 갈 때면 집에서 일찍 출발해요.
expressions.english: I walk very slowly whenever I go somewhere, so I leave the house early.
expressions.notes:
어디 = where; somewhere
-(으)면 = whenever + S + V
집 = house; home
일찍 = early; soon
출발하다 = to depart

expressions.korean: 일본어를 배운 지 1주일 밖에 안 돼서 아직 일본어 읽는 속도가 많이 느려요.
expressions.english: It's only been a week since I started learning Japanese, therefore I'm still slow at reading it.
expressions.notes:
일본어 = the Japanese language
배우다 = to learn; to study
-(으)ㄴ, X = (to have been a certain amount of time) since + S + V
1주일 = one week
밖에 = just, only
안 = not
~ 밖에 안 되다 = It's only been + (time); It's only been + (time) + since ...
아직 = (not) yet
읽다 = to read

expressions.korean: 저는 밥 먹는 속도가 느려서 남들이 밥 먹으면서 대화를 할 때 저는 조용히 먹 기만 해요.
expressions.english: I eat slowly, so when people have a conversation while eating, I just eat quietly.
expressions.notes:
남 = other people; others
-(으)면서 = while + S + V-ing
대화 = conversation
대화를 하다 = to have a conversation; to talk
조용하다 = to be quiet
조용히 is an adverb form of 조용하다.
-기만 하다 = just + V; to only + V
